title: runtime SDK CURSOR_API_KEY AUTOPILOT_MODEL
purpose: I2.2 defines runtime sdk cursor api key autopilot model for the agent-driven expert system. Runtime & integration — IDE, SDK, headless, MCP tools.
scope: Owns `I2.2` only; siblings under `I2` must not duplicate this spec.
Aligns with minimal HITL: H1 plan, H2 blocker, H3 sign-off ([INTRO-1.2](INTRO-1.2-human-touchpoint-contract-h1-h2-h3.md)).
Conflicts resolve in favor of [Vision §11 — Branch I — Runtime & integration plane](../../full-automation-vision-and-hierarchy.md#11-branch-i-runtime-integration-plane).
behavior_steps: Before [I2.1](I2.1-runtime-sdk-run-local-pipeline-goal-autopilot.md) invokes Cursor CLI or cursor-sdk for a headless pursuit turn, preflight loads `CURSOR_API_KEY` from environment or the operator secrets store listed in docs/facts/INDEX—never from committed repo files.

Optional `AUTOPILOT_MODEL` overrides the default slug for autopilot batches, but [J1](J1-model-policy.md) and model-policy.json still bind conductor roles to genius tier and worker roles to economy—override cannot bypass subagent_models routing.

Each SDK daemon wake passes the resolved API key and model selection into Agent.prompt or headless CLI (`agent -p`); authentication failures abort the turn immediately with structured H2 citing credential setup steps.

Credential rotation mid-pursuit requires the operator to update environment or the secrets store; the next daemon wake reloads config, dual-writes the change to journal and state.json, and resumes only after preflight re-authenticates.

Missing or invalid credentials never fall back to unauthenticated API calls—pursuit stops fail-closed with H2 and may notify via [I5.2](I5.2-runtime-notify-webhook-email-h2-h3-only.md) rather than silently retrying.

narrative: I2.2 defines runtime sdk cursor api key autopilot model for the agent-driven expert system. Runtime & integration — IDE, SDK, headless, MCP tools. In practice, the system before [i2.1](i2.1-runtime-sdk-run-local-pipeline-goal-autopilot.md) invokes cursor cli or cursor-sdk for a headless pursuit turn, preflight loads `cursor_api_key` from environment or the operator secrets store listed in docs/facts/index—never from committed repo files, then optional `autopilot_model` overrides the default slug for autopilot batches, but [j1](j1-model-policy.md) and model-policy.json still bind conductor roles to genius tier and worker roles to economy—override cannot bypass subagent_models routing.
reader_narrative:
<!-- prose-source: agent plane-i 2026-06-28 -->

SDK runtime requires `CURSOR_API_KEY` and optional `AUTOPILOT_MODEL` override for headless or remote worker sessions. Secrets never commit to repo; operators configure via environment or local secrets store documented in facts INDEX.

Model override must still conform to model-policy.json tier mapping—daemon does not bypass genius/economy routing for conductor vs worker roles. Missing credentials produce structured H2 with setup instructions, not silent fallback to unauthenticated calls.
